summary: Studies dropout as a tool to reduce underfitting. Proposes early dropout (active only in initial training phases) to improve model fit, and late dropout to prevent overfitting. Analyzes gradient dynamics and provides comprehensive empirical evaluation on ImageNet-1K with Vision Transformers.

modules.name: EarlyDropout
modules.description: Dropout applied only during early training epochs to reduce underfitting

modules.name: LateDropout
modules.description: Dropout applied only in later training to prevent overfitting

equations.latex: GDV = \frac{1}{|G| (|G|-1)} \sum_{g_i, g_j \in G, i < j} \frac{1}{2} (1 - \cos(g_i, g_j))

training.data_augmentation: Random crops, horizontal flips, color jittering, elastic deformations

assumptions: Gradient direction variance metric assumes normalized gradients
Early/late dropout effectiveness depends on model size and dataset
ViT-T/S/B model variants tested; scaling assumptions explicit
ambiguities: Optimal dropout scheduling curve may vary by architecture
Computational cost analysis incomplete for very large models
